name: superset
description: Superset dashboard availability. Superset exposes no Datadog integration of its own (the chart wires statsd + /health, nothing to DD), so availability is derived from kube-state-metrics on the superset web Deployment. This is a low-urgency (P2) signal: a downed dashboard is recoverable and not user-data-impacting. NOTE: the generic kubernetes 'deployment-replica-mismatch' rule (k8s.json) also covers this Deployment at CRITICAL — this rule adds the matrix's superset-scoped P2 view.

datadog.message: [P2] Superset has no available web replicas on cluster {{kube_cluster_name.name}} — the dashboard UI is unavailable. Recoverable; check the superset-{{env}} deployment, its DB (superset-postgres) and redis.

annotations.description: The Superset web deployment has zero available replicas, so dashboards are unreachable. Recoverable operational issue — check pod status, the postgres backend, and redis.
